mnemonics.title: DAB CABS - Bus Types
mnemonics.content: DAB CABS stands for 'Data bus, Address bus, Control bus' - The three essential buses that connect components. Remember: DAB means 'Don't Forget These CABS' (Components Always Bring Signals)

mnemonics.title: HAMP - Control Unit Types
mnemonics.content: HAMP stands for 'Hardwired And MicroProgrammed' control. Remember: 'HAMP' sounds like 'hamp' meaning something restricted - choose the right control method for your processor!

mnemonics.title: MAR- PC - IR Journey
mnemonics.content: MAR gets PC's address, loads to IR for decoding. Mnemonic: 'Mothers Always Purr When Infants Read' - Mother (MAR) takes address from Father (PC), Infant (IR) reads and processes

mnemonics.title: BRW - Bus Read/Write
mnemonics.content: BRW = Bus Read/Write signals. Remember: 'Buses Really Work' when READ and WRITE signals are properly controlled

mnemonics.title: CU Functions
mnemonics.content: Control Unit: 'Timing Tells Instructions To Execute' - Timing, Tells/Generates control signals, Instructions fetch/decode, To Execute operations. Each letter: T-Timing, T-Tells, I-Instructions, T-To, E-Execute

acronyms.usage: The component that directs operation of the processor, generates control signals to execute instructions
Performs arithmetic and logical operations as directed by CU
Holds the memory address from which data will be fetched or to which data will be stored

acronyms.expansion: Memory Buffer Register / Memory Data Register
acronyms.usage: Holds the data being transferred to/from memory
Contains address of next instruction to be fetched
General purpose register for arithmetic operations
Defines how software interacts with the bus and hardware
Allows peripheral devices to access memory directly, bypassing CPU control
Used in hardwired control unit design for sequencing control signals

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a multi-lane highway: Data bus = trucks carrying cargo (data), Address bus = GPS/signage showing destination addresses, Control bus = traffic police/信号灯 controlling when trucks can move, stop, or deliver

Picture CU as an orchestra conductor: Each musician (ALU, registers, memory) must play at the right time. The conductor's baton (control signals) coordinates every section - this is exactly how CU coordinates CPU components

Visualize a factory assembly line: Fetch (go to warehouse/PC) → Decode (read instruction/IR) → Execute (do the work/ALU) → Store (put results away/memory). Each step needs control signals like assembly instructions

visualTips.concept: Bus Arbitration as Round Table Meeting
visualTips.visualization: Multiple devices want to use the bus (speak). Picture knights (devices) at a round table - they need arbitration (who speaks first). Priority-based or daisy-chain arbitration = knight with highest rank speaks first

Think of a recipe book: Each instruction (dish) is made of microinstructions (steps). Control memory stores all recipes. Hardwired = one-person doing everything instinctively; Microprogrammed = following recipe cards

analogies.analogy: Like delivery trucks on a road - they carry packages (data) in both directions between cities (CPU and memory/peripherals). Width of road = number of bits carried
Like house addresses on letters - they don't carry people/data, they only tell WHERE the data should go. One-way street from CPU to memory
Like traffic lights and road signs - they don't carry passengers or specify destinations, but control WHEN things happen (read, write, clock signals)
Like a mechanical watch with gears - each gear position directly causes specific movements. Fast but inflexible; changes require redesigning gears (rewiring)
Like a programmable coffee machine - you can change recipes (microprograms) by changing the settings. Slower but flexible; updates just mean changing the program, not the machine
Like students wanting to speak in a classroom - some use 'raise hand' (request), teacher decides who speaks (arbitration), then grants permission (grant signal). Priority = class monitor goes first
Like a railway signal system - signals tell the train (data) when to move, stop, or switch tracks. Green = go, Red = stop, Yellow = slow/wait
